news_items.headline: Retail firms warn of price hikes if Iran war extends for months
news_items.description: Fashion retailer Next has accounted for millions in additional costs likely to arise from the Middle East conflict

news_items.headline: Iran war will spare no major economy, says OECD — but the UK is more vulnerable than others
news_items.description: The U.K. is predicted to be worse hit by the Iran war than its developed market counterparts, the OECD said in its interim economic outlook on Thursday.

news_items.headline: Higher gas prices from Iran war could offset bigger tax refunds from Trump's 'big beautiful bill'
news_items.description: Amid the Iran war, higher gas prices could offset the bigger tax refunds from President Donald Trump's

news_items.headline: Trump says Iranian negotiators 'better get serious soon, before it is too late'
news_items.description: Trump's comments come amid ongoing confusion over whether the U.S. and Iran have held negotiations over ending the war.

news_items.headline: What comes next? Three attack scenarios as U.S. sends thousands more troops to Middle East
news_items.description: One of Iran's top lawmakers has said that they were anticipating a potential ground invasion of one its islands.

news_items.headline: Gulf states say they're ready for 'self defense' as stance shifts on Iran war
news_items.description: Gulf states have issued a joint statement on Thursday condemning Iran's

news_items.headline: Electronic warfare in the Persian Gulf: How GPS interference is disrupting the Middle East
news_items.description: Since the start of the U.S.-Israel war with Iran on Feb. 28, interference with location-based services has disrupted life across the Persian Gulf.

news_items.headline: Trump wants to squeeze Iran into peace talks with more troops — but it may backfire, analysts say
news_items.description: Trump's threats to ramp up military presence may be to pressure Iran to negotiate but the battle may be harder than he calculated, say analysts.

news_items.headline: European markets head for lower open amid Iran peace talks uncertainty
news_items.description: European stocks are expected to open in negative territory on Thursday as investors weigh mixed messages on the status of Middle East peace talks.

news_items.headline: Asia markets set to fall as Iran rules out direct U.S. talks despite reviewing proposal
news_items.description: Asia-Pacific markets looked set to mostly fall after Iran signaled it had no intention of holding direct talks with the United States.
